input.prompt: The user wants to connect their macOS Claude Code CLI session to their phone. They ran a "/mobile" command which returned no content. How does Claude Code's mobile/phone connection feature work? Is there a way to monitor or interact with a Claude Code session from a mobile device? Search for any documentation about mobile, phone, remote access, QR codes, or similar features in Claude Code.

content.text:
Perfect! I now have the complete information. Let me provide you with a comprehensive answer.

## Claude Code Mobile Connection: Remote Control

The `/mobile` command you ran is actually documented in the official Claude Code docs. Here's what it does and how the feature works:

### What `/mobile` Does

According to the official documentation, **if you don't have the Claude app yet, use the `/mobile` command inside Claude Code to display a download QR code** for [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) or [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude).

The fact that it returned no content suggests either:
1. You already have the Claude mobile app installed, so there's nothing to download
2. The command returned a QR code that the terminal couldn't render properly in your environment

### How to Connect Your macOS Session to Your Phone

Claude Code has a built-in feature called **Remote Control** (shipped February 25, 2026) that lets you control a local CLI session from your phone. Here's how to use it:

**Option 1: Start a new Remote Control session**
```bash
claude remote-control
```
This keeps running in your terminal and displays a session URL. Press spacebar to show a QR code that you can scan with the Claude mobile app on your phone.

**Option 2: Enable it for an existing session**
If you're already in a Claude Code session, run:
```
/remote-control
```
or the shorthand:
```
/rc
```

### Key Features

- **Local execution**: Your session runs entirely on your Mac — nothing moves to the cloud
- **Full access**: Your filesystem, MCP servers, tools, and all project configuration stay available
- **Synchronized**: The conversation syncs across your Mac terminal, browser, and phone so you can send messages from any device
- **Automatic reconnection**: If your laptop sleeps or network drops, the session reconnects when your machine comes back online
- **QR code access**: Scan the QR code shown in your terminal to open the session directly in the Claude mobile app
- **Browser access**: You can also open the session URL in any web browser at `claude.ai/code`

### Requirements

- Claude Code v2.1.51 or later (`claude --version` to check)
- A claude.ai account (not API key authentication)
- Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise subscription
- Workspace trust (run `claude` in your project directory at least once)

### Optional: Enable for All Sessions

You can make Remote Control automatically activate for every interactive session by running:
```
/config
```
Then toggle **Enable Remote Control for all sessions** to `true`.

---

**Sources:**
- [Continue local sessions from any device with Remote Control - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control)
- [Claude Code Remote Control: Run Your Terminal from Your Phone (2026 Guide) | NxCode](https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/claude-code-remote-control-mobile-terminal-handoff-guide-2026)
- [Anthropic's Remote Control feature brings Claude Code to mobile devices - Help Net Security](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/02/25/anthropic-remote-control-claude-code-feature/)

Use the **Remote Control** feature to connect your Mac session to your phone:

**In your current session**, run:
```
/remote-control
```
(shorthand: `/rc`)

This displays a QR code in your terminal. Scan it with the Claude mobile app ([iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) / [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude)) to connect.

**Key details:**

message.content: How do I get qr code into my ios claude app?

content.text:
Once you run `/rc` (or `/remote-control`) in your terminal:

1. **A QR code appears in your terminal** — if it doesn't show automatically, press **spacebar** to display it
2. **Open the Claude app on your iPhone**
3. **Scan the QR code** using your iPhone's camera app or the built-in scanner in the Claude app — it opens directly into the connected session

Alternatively, the terminal also shows a **URL** you can copy and open in Safari on your phone, which will redirect into the Claude app.

If the QR code isn't rendering properly in your terminal (some terminal configurations don't display block characters well), try:
- Zooming out (Cmd + minus) so the QR code fits
- Using the URL instead

message.content: my claude app starts but doesn't control my session.

content.text:
A few things to check:

1. **Same account** — Make sure you're signed into the same claude.ai account on both your Mac and the iOS app

2. **Authentication method** — Remote Control requires a **claude.ai login**, not an API key. Check with:
   ```
   claude config get authMethod
   ```
   If it shows `api-key`, you'll need to switch to OAuth login

3. **Claude Code version** — Needs v2.1.51+:
   ```
   claude --version
   ```

4. **Is the session actually active?** — After running `/rc`, the terminal should show a status line indicating it's waiting for connections. If it said nothing or errored, the feature may not be enabled.

5. **On your phone** — After scanning, you should see the active session listed in the Claude app. You may need to explicitly tap on it to connect — it doesn't always auto-attach.

Which of these might apply to your situation? Specifically — what do you see on the phone after scanning the QR code?

message.content: Remot Control Active displayed on macos.  No sign of a session on ios: I am logged in oauth there.

input.prompt: A user has Claude Code Remote Control active on their Mac (it shows "Remote Control Active" in the terminal). They have the iOS Claude app and are logged in with OAuth (same account). But the iOS app shows NO sign of the remote session — nothing appears. How does the iOS app surface remote control sessions? Is there a specific tab, menu, or section they need to navigate to? Does the app need a minimum version? Are there any known issues with Remote Control not appearing on mobile? Search for troubleshooting steps specific to this scenario.
